channel.title: Linux on k4i's blog
channel.description: Recent content in Linux on k4i's blog

item.title: SSH Port Forwarding: Local and Remote Tunnels Explained
item.author: sky_io@outlook.com (K4i)
item.description:
SSH port forwarding (also called SSH tunneling) lets you securely route network traffic through an encrypted SSH connection. There are two directions: local forwarding (-L) to access remote services, and remote forwarding (-R) to expose local services to a remote machine.

Remote port forwarding (-R)

Remote port forwarding forwards a port on the remote machine to a specified address and port on the local machine.
item.dc:creator: K4i

item.title: Fix boot problem after updating packages
item.author: sky_io@outlook.com (K4i)
item.description:
Introduction

Just the other day I updated all my packages through yay -Syu (like pacman -Syu but also updates aur packages).

And after a reboot, it entered a boot loop…

I believe this had happened to most arch users, and most of the time its just because we broke the dependencies of some packages after the upgrade.

So here’s how I saved boot failure after yay -Syu.

You’ll need a bootable usb stick (preferable the one you use for installing the system).

Manually boot from grub (optional)

follow this guide1 to boot your linux from grub (generates log).
item.dc:creator: K4i
